pattern: Sorting numbers into three buckets by remainder mod 3 reduces every valid triplet shape to just four fixed remainder patterns, so only the largest few values in each bucket ever need to be examined.
hints: Split the numbers into three groups by their remainder when divided by 3, and sort each group so its largest values come first.
A triplet's sum is divisible by 3 in exactly two shapes: three numbers from the very same remainder group, or one number from each of the three groups.

For the same-group shape only the top three values of that group can ever matter, and for the mixed shape only the single largest value of each group matters -- compare the best achievable sum across all of these candidate shapes.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two, no valid triplet exists
exactly three elements that already work
only the same-remainder shape is available, mixed triplets are impossible
one remainder group is completely empty, so a mixed triplet is impossible
the best same-group triplet beats the best mixed triplet, not the other way around
exactly three elements at the maximum allowed value
smallest possible values with a valid mixed triplet

stress.description: random arrays across the full value and length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: values skewed toward a single remainder class, stressing the same-group triplet path
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.choice([3, 6, 9, 12]) * rng.randint(1, 8333) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
